git_rules.merge_to_main: ONLY when score is confirmed better than previous best — ask user before merging

leaderboard_strategy.rule: Later rounds may be weighted more heavily on the leaderboard
leaderboard_strategy.implication: Round 1 is for learning — get a submission in, collect /analysis ground truth, tune the model. Don't sacrifice learning for a marginally better Round 1 score.
leaderboard_strategy.round_1_goal: Working submission + ground truth collection via /analysis
leaderboard_strategy.round_2_goal: Apply transition matrix + tuned α → meaningful score improvement
leaderboard_strategy.merge_to_main_rule: Only when score is verified better than previous best

key_insights.static_cells: Mountain (5) and Ocean (10) NEVER change — predict with ~0.99 confidence using initial_states (free)
key_insights.initial_states_free: GET /rounds/{id} gives full initial grid + settlements for all 5 seeds at zero query cost
key_insights.ground_truth_is_distribution: Ground truth is itself a H×W×6 probability tensor from Monte Carlo — we're estimating a distribution not a single outcome
key_insights.zero_probability_kills_score: qᵢ=0 when pᵢ>0 → KL=infinity → always apply 0.01 floor then renormalize
key_insights.static_cells_excluded: Ocean and Mountain have ~zero entropy → excluded from scoring weight — focus budget on dynamic cells
key_insights.budget_api: GET /budget returns queries_used — sync with API before each query

steps.description: Project scaffold — config.py, requirements.txt, __init__.py files, folder structure.
Read all API docs — Simulation Mechanics, API Endpoints, Scoring, Quickstart. Fully documented in PROBLEM.md. All terrain types, scoring formula, endpoints, rate limits confirmed.
key_discoveries: 8 internal terrain codes → 6 prediction classes (Ocean+Plains+Empty all → class 0)
Mountain and Ocean are STATIC — deterministic predictions possible from initial_states
Score formula: max(0, min(100, 100 × exp(-3 × weighted_kl))). Uniform ≈ 1-5/100
Static cells excluded from scoring weight — focus queries on dynamic zones
GET /budget endpoint — check remaining queries without tracking locally
GET /analysis post-round — gives ground truth H×W×6 tensor for learning

steps.description: API client setup — src/api/client.py: typed Session wrapper with Bearer auth, methods for all endpoints (get_rounds, get_round_detail, get_budget, simulate, submit). src/api/models.py: dataclasses for all request/response types.
steps.note: No raw requests calls anywhere else in codebase — all HTTP goes through client.py

steps.description: scripts/api_discovery.py — minimal probe script: authenticate, GET /rounds to find active round, GET /rounds/{id} to see initial_states structure, GET /budget to confirm remaining queries. Print all raw responses. Zero simulate calls — costs nothing.
steps.budget_impact: 0 queries — no simulate calls, just reading free endpoints
steps.verify: Can see round_id, initial grid codes (10/11/0 for ocean/plains/empty, 5 for mountain), settlement list, queries_used

steps.description: Initial state analyzer — src/model/initial_analyzer.py: parse initial_states for all 5 seeds. Classify every cell as: STATIC_MOUNTAIN (code 5), STATIC_OCEAN (code 10), DYNAMIC_SETTLEMENT (code 1 or 2), DYNAMIC_LAND (code 0 or 11), STATIC_FOREST (code 4). Build per-seed 40×40 certainty map.
steps.why: Mountain and Ocean = near-certain predictions at zero query cost. Identify EXACTLY which cells need observation budget.
steps.output: Per-seed: list of static cells (high confidence), list of dynamic cells (need queries)

steps.description: Query Planner — src/observation/query_planner.py: given dynamic cell map, plan viewport queries. Strategy: 9 non-overlapping 15×15 tiles = full map scan (45 queries), 5 spare queries for re-querying highest-entropy dynamic zones. Assign queries across seeds evenly.
steps.math: Anchors at [0,15,25] for both x and y — NOT [0,15,30]. Each tile is a full 15×15. Tiles at 25 overlap [25-29] with previous tile but guarantee full map coverage. 9×5=45 + 5 spare = 50 exactly.
steps.why_not_30: x=30 + w=15 = x=45, out of bounds on a 40-wide map. x=25 + w=15 = x=40, exactly fits.
steps.future_optimization: Entropy-guided query placement after initial scan (see features_backlog)

steps.description: Observation Runner — src/observation/runner.py: calls GET /budget first, executes query plan one at a time, saves raw response to data/observations/seed{seed}_x{x}_y{y}_{timestamp}.json after each call. Respects 5 req/sec rate limit with sleep(0.2).
steps.critical: Save to disk IMMEDIATELY after each query. If crash at query 43, keep all 43. Sync with GET /budget before starting.
steps.parsing_gotcha: response.width/height = full map (40×40), NOT viewport size. response.grid shape = viewport_h × viewport_w. Use response.viewport.x and .y to place cells onto the full 40×40 tensor.

steps.description: Data Analysis — scripts/analyze_observations.py: load all saved observations, print terrain frequency per class, print ASCII coverage map, show per-cell observation count, identify highest-variance cells (most uncertain = most important to score well on).
steps.output: Print: coverage map, terrain distribution, top-10 most uncertain cells

steps.description: Terrain Estimator — src/model/terrain_estimator.py: build per-cell H×W×6 probability distributions using 3 stacked layers. KEY FIX: n=1 observation is NOT treated as an empirical distribution — it is a Bayesian update on the spatial prior.
steps.n1_problem: Full scan gives each cell exactly 1 observation per seed. Seeds have different maps — cannot pool across seeds. Treating n=1 as 95% confidence is a delta function, not a distribution. Fix: Bayesian smoothing.

LAYER_A_static.what: Cells classified STATIC from initial_states (Mountain code 5, Ocean code 10)
LAYER_A_static.floor: 1e-5 — these cells excluded from scoring weight anyway

LAYER_B_bayesian.what: Cells we observed once via simulate() — Bayesian update on spatial prior
LAYER_B_bayesian.alpha: α = 0.55 (default) — how much weight to give our single observation vs prior
LAYER_B_bayesian.alpha_rationale: With n=1, we trust our observation more than pure prior but acknowledge high uncertainty. Tune this after Round 1 analysis.
LAYER_B_bayesian.example: Prior=[0.30,0.20,0.20,0.10,0.15,0.05], observe class 2 (Port) → posterior≈[0.14,0.09,0.56,0.05,0.07,0.02] — Port boosted but real uncertainty remains
LAYER_B_bayesian.floor: 0.01 floor on all 6 classes, then renormalize
LAYER_B_bayesian.priority: MIDDLE — overrides Layer C, overridden by Layer A

LAYER_C_spatial_prior.what: Fallback for any unobserved cells (shouldn't exist with full scan, but kept as safety net)
LAYER_C_spatial_prior.base: Global observed terrain frequency across all queries as starting distribution
rules: Adjacent to initial Settlement (code 1): +0.15 P(class1), +0.10 P(class2), +0.08 P(class3) — expansion zone
Coastal cell + near initial Settlement: +0.20 P(class2 Port) — ports need coast access
Adjacent to Mountain (code 5): -0.10 P(class1 Settlement) — mountains block expansion
Distance > 5 cells from ANY initial settlement: +0.15 P(class0), +0.10 P(class4) — frontier stays wild
Initial Forest (code 4) isolated from settlements: +0.20 P(class4) — forest is mostly static
LAYER_C_spatial_prior.how: Apply rules additively. Clip to [0,1]. Apply 0.01 floor. Renormalize.
LAYER_C_spatial_prior.also_used_as: The prior fed into Layer B's Bayesian update — so Layer C always runs first

steps.order_of_operations: 1. Compute Layer C spatial prior for every cell. 2. For observed cells: Layer B Bayesian update using Layer C as prior. 3. For static cells: override with Layer A. 4. Apply floors. 5. Renormalize.

tunable_params.alpha: 0.55 — controls single-observation confidence. Tune post-Round 1 using learning_log.
tunable_params.spatial_boost_weights: The +0.15/+0.10/+0.08 values in Layer C rules — tune from round history.
tunable_params.floor_dynamic: 0.01 — tune down if scores show we're over-smoothing.

steps.description: Tensor Builder — src/prediction/tensor_builder.py: assemble H×W×6 numpy array from estimator. Hard assertion: every row sums to 1.0 ± 1e-6 AFTER floor application. Save to data/predictions/seed{seed}_tensor.npy and .json.

main.py — full pipeline orchestration: load config → auth → get round → analyze initial states → plan queries → run observations → estimate terrain → build tensors → submit all 5 seeds. Clear function per stage. Prints budget remaining after every query.
Submit & Score — POST /submit for all 5 seeds. Record score in data/scores.json. If better than previous best: propose PR victor → main (ask user first). If not: loop back to Step 9.
steps.branch_rule: No push to remote. No merge to main. PR only when score confirmed better than previous best — always ask user.

steps.description: Post-Round Analysis & Learning Log — scripts/post_round_analysis.py: fetch ground truth via GET /analysis, compare cell-by-cell against our predictions, write findings to data/round_history/ and learning_log.md. This is how we improve each round.

what_to_track.per_cell_kl: Which cells hurt us most? → tells us where Layer C rules are wrong
what_to_track.per_class_error: Did we systematically underpredict Ruin? Overpredict Port? → adjust Layer C boosts
what_to_track.per_initial_code_error: Cells starting as Settlement → what did they actually become? → builds transition matrix
what_to_track.alpha_calibration: Was our α=0.55 too confident or not confident enough? → tune for next round
what_to_track.regional_patterns: Are we worse inland vs coastal? Near mountains? → refine spatial rules
what_to_track.score_history: Track score per round to see if we're actually improving

steps.learning_log_format: Append one entry per round. Max 5 bullet points per round. Focus on surprises — things that were systematically wrong, not random errors.
steps.feeds_into: Round 2: update α and Layer C boost weights. Round 3+: replace Layer C with data-driven transition matrix from accumulated round_history.
